name: multiple-agents-same-workspace
description: DIFFERENT coding agents (claudecode + codex + pi) live in the SAME cwd at once. The daemon labels each adapter independently (disjoint ProcessMatchers + transcript roots; per-(adapter, session_id) state and metrics from the maps.go projections), so the single recording shows distinct sessions each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. Recorded by run-cell-multi.sh, which launches each listed adapter's interactive driver concurrently against one shared cwd under a single --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each (same workspace events.jsonl, that adapter's own transcript). Each by_adapter cell is driven by the SAME recording — each stores its adapter's transcript for the replay golden and shares the workspace events.jsonl. The spec is adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to enter working, agentB = the other), so any two of the coexisting sessions validate the two-distinct-independent-arcs assertion; a third coexisting adapter is tolerated (working anchors + new_session skip placeholders and already-matched sessions).

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

This scenario is about COEXISTENCE: one Aider session and one different-adapter session (e.g. claudecode or codex) running concurrently in the SAME project directory, each shown with its correct adapter label and fully independent state + metrics. The spec is adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to enter working, agentB = the other), so aider can play either role; a third coexisting adapter is tolerated.

### agent_supports: yes

Aider is an ordinary CLI: an OS `python` process invoking the aider script that edits files in the current working directory. It has no notion of, and imposes no constraint on, what other agent CLIs run in the same cwd. Crucially, aider does NOT write under a shared `$HOME` root — it materializes its chat history per-project in the cwd as `.aider.chat.history.md` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go`, `transcriptFilename = ".aider.chat.history.md"`). So launching a second, unrelated agent (claude, codex, pi) in the same directory cannot interfere with a running aider session. The agent trivially "supports" coexistence — there is nothing for it to support or block. The scenario's `requires: [headless_mode]` gate is satisfied (`replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` → `headless_mode: true`).

### daemon_capability: full

The daemon labels each session by its adapter INDEPENDENTLY, by construction — and aider's transport is disjoint from every other adapter's on all three axes:

1. **Per-adapter process matching.** Aider registers `Process.Match = CommandPattern{Regex: /aider}` (`agent.go`) — distinct from the `ExactName` matchers of codex (`codex`), pi (`pi`), and from claudecode's matcher. The process scanner runs every adapter's matcher separately (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/maps.go` → `ProcessNames` / `PIDDiscoverers` projections), so an aider process is tagged `aider` from which matcher hit, never from the cwd. The `/aider` command-pattern anchor specifically excludes wrappers (tmux, sh) that merely mention aider in their argv.

2. **Disjoint transcript transport.** Aider is the only adapter using `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (`agent.go`), whereas claudecode/codex/pi all use `FilesUnderRoot` under fixed `$HOME`-relative dirs (`~/.claude/projects/`, `~/.codex/sessions/`, `~/.pi/agent/sessions/`). Aider's per-CWD markdown transcript can never collide with another adapter's JSONL-under-root transcript, even when sharing a cwd. The shared-file conflation risk only applies to TWO aider processes in the same cwd (the distinct `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` scenario), not here: the coexisting session is a DIFFERENT adapter writing to a different transport entirely.

3. **Per-(adapter, session_id) state + metrics.** Each running aider process gets its OWN fresh `Parser` instance (`agent.go`: `NewParser: func() agent.RawParser { return &Parser{} }`) precisely so aider's idle-flush turn-tracking state never collides across concurrent processes; the PID owning the session is bound by cwd + the `/aider` command-line match (`pid.go`, `DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine`). The aider parser emits a clean per-session arc — `user_message` on a `#### ` prompt, `assistant_message` on each `> Tokens:` model-call line, and `turn_done` synthesized by `IdleFlush` after 1500ms quiet — driving only the aider session's working->ready transition. A turn in a coexisting claude/codex session drives a different transcript via a different parser and cannot be attributed to the aider row, and vice versa.

Because each adapter's matcher + watch transport + parser are wired separately off the `allAgents` projections (`maps.go`), independent labeling and metrics fall out of the architecture rather than needing special-case code — the same property the codex/claudecode/pi rows of this scenario already rely on. Aider's only structural difference (FilesUnderCWD markdown vs FilesUnderRoot JSONL) makes its transport MORE disjoint from the others, not less, so the no-cross-contamination guarantee holds at least as strongly. Aider has no subagents (`capabilities.json` → `subagents: false`; no `SubagentCounter` registered in `maps.go`), so the "no cross-contamination of subagent counts" assertion is trivially satisfied for the aider row.

### driver_capability: ready

The recipe `script` is `send` → `wait_turn` → `sleep` (`scenarios.json`), and the aider interactive driver (`scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`) implements all three step types. This cell runs ONE aider session next to a DIFFERENT adapter, so the single-session driver (send / wait_turn / sleep) is sufficient — it does NOT need the multi-session `session` step that `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` would require. No gapped primitive (restart / reset_session / fork / session / keys) appears in the recipe, so the driver is ready.

## Note on recording

This cell has no aider recording yet. The scenario's `cross_adapter` list in `scenarios.json` is `[claudecode, codex, pi]` and `by_adapter` has rows only for those three — aider is NOT in the launched-driver set that `run-cell-multi.sh` spins up. The single shared recording (workspace `events.jsonl` + per-adapter transcript) already covers claudecode/codex/pi. Adding aider as a coexisting adapter is a recording-rig task (add aider to `cross_adapter[]` and give it a `by_adapter` row + interactive driver invocation), NOT an observability or driver gap — the verdict above is independent of whether a recording exists. The aider driver itself is ready; the rig's adapter set is what needs extending.
caveats: No aider recording exists for this cell yet: aider is absent from the scenario's cross_adapter[] list ([claudecode, codex, pi]) and from by_adapter, so run-cell-multi.sh does not launch an aider driver. Recording requires extending the rig (add aider to cross_adapter[] + a by_adapter row) — this is a recording-rig set gap, not a daemon observability gap nor an aider-driver step gap.

Independent labeling/metrics rely on the daemon scanning each adapter's process matcher and transcript transport separately. Aider's transport is doubly disjoint (CommandPattern /aider matcher + per-CWD markdown FilesUnderCWD), so the assumption holds strongly; it would only need re-checking if a future adapter shared the /aider command pattern or the .aider.chat.history.md filename — not the case today.

Aider's working->ready arc closes via IdleFlush after a 1500ms quiet window (effectively ~5s given the SessionDetector's staleWorkingRefreshInterval), unlike adapters with an explicit end-of-turn marker. This affects only the timing of aider's own ready transition, not whether the two arcs stay independent.

sources.note: Aider registers Process.Match = CommandPattern{Regex: /aider} and Source = FilesUnderCWD{Filename: .aider.chat.history.md, Parser: RawLineParser with a fresh per-process NewParser} — per-adapter matcher + per-CWD markdown transport, disjoint from every FilesUnderRoot adapter
AdapterName/ProcessName = aider — distinct label and process name from claudecode/codex/pi/opencode
DiscoverPID binds the owning aider PID by cwd + the /aider command-line match (DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine) — the session is keyed to its own process, not to the shared cwd

Parser emits user_message / assistant_message / tool_result and synthesizes turn_done via IdleFlush (1500ms) — a clean per-session working->ready arc; comment documents the per-process Parser instance so idle-flush state never collides across concurrent aider processes

ProcessNames / PIDDiscoverers / Parsers / SubagentCounters / MetricsProviders are all per-adapter-name projections of the allAgents slice — independent labeling and metrics fall out of the wiring; aider registers no SubagentCounter
headless_mode: true (the scenario's requires gate is satisfied); subagents: false (no subagent-count cross-contamination possible for the aider row)

multiple-agents-same-workspace: recipe script = send/wait_turn/sleep (all implemented by drive-aider-interactive.sh); cross_adapter = [claudecode, codex, pi] and by_adapter has rows only for those three; spec is adapter-agnostic (agentA/agentB, third adapter tolerated) so aider is an eligible coexisting adapter but is not yet in the launched-driver set
Peer assessment for the same scenario in the new two-axis schema (codex yes/full/ready, conf 0.95) — anchors the per-adapter-independence reasoning that this aider assessment generalizes

preconditions: aider AND a different-adapter CLI (claude, and optionally codex/pi — the other concurrent adapters) on PATH and authenticated; aider needs a provider key/login (it is NOT auth-set-up in the onboarding rig environment, so this cell records only where aider auth is available)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other aider/claudecode session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches each cross_adapter[] driver concurrently, all pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (the aider interactive driver honors $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD so it shares the workspace with the other adapters; aider writes .aider.chat.history.md IN that shared cwd)

aider registers Process.Match=CommandPattern{/aider} + Source=FilesUnderCWD{Filename:.aider.chat.history.md} with a fresh stateful Parser per process — doubly disjoint from claudecode ~/.claude/projects, codex ~/.codex/sessions, and pi ~/.pi/agent/sessions (different matcher AND per-CWD markdown rather than FilesUnderRoot JSONL) — so the daemon keys the aider session independently; its single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep) is sufficient because aider runs ONE session next to a DIFFERENT adapter (distinct from multiple-sessions-same-cwd, which needs two SAME-adapter sessions)
this cell stores the aider .aider.chat.history.md transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (every coexisting adapter)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: delta

verify: events.jsonl contains the aider session (label aider) and at least one different-adapter session, each keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other (aiders fresh per-process parser + per-CWD markdown transport guarantee no shared state)
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the aider row into another adapters; aider has no subagents so its subagent count stays absent

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

This cell is really an irrlicht-dispatcher test that happens to be filed under the claudecode adapter. The scenario co-launches a `claude` and a non-claude (e.g. `codex`) process in the same cwd and asserts that irrlicht reports two rows under their correct adapter labels with no cross-contamination.

irrlicht's design isolates adapters at three layers, all of which are exercised here:
1. **Process matching** — each adapter declares its own `ProcessMatcher` (claudecode: `ExactName{Name: "claude"}`; codex: `ExactName{Name: "codex"}`). The process scanner runs each matcher independently and tags each PID with the originating adapter name.
2. **Transcript roots** — adapters declare disjoint `FilesUnderRoot.Dir` paths (claudecode: `~/.claude/projects/`; codex: `~/.codex/sessions/`). The fswatcher attaches a per-root parser-factory derived from `Parsers(allAgents)` in `maps.go`, so a JSONL line under the claudecode root can never be fed to the codex parser.
3. **Per-session metrics** — `SessionState` carries an adapter-name field; metrics, subagent counts, and parent linkage all key off the (adapter, session_id) tuple. There is no global per-cwd aggregation in the domain.

Spec bullets map cleanly:
- 'Each session appears with its correct adapter label' — adapter is recorded at presession_created via the matcher-tagged scan result.
- 'States transition independently per agent' — the two tailers are different goroutines reading different files; the state classifier is per-session.
- 'No cross-contamination of metrics, parent linkage, or subagent counts' — subagent counters are registered per adapter name in `SubagentCounters(allAgents)` and only invoked for sessions of that adapter.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

irrlicht has no first-class notion of a 'workspace' — there is no row that says 'cwd X has these N agents'. The dashboard groups by cwd visually, but the daemon's session list is flat. That's correct for this scenario: the spec asks only about per-session correctness, not aggregate workspace state.

## Recording (2026-05-24)

Recorded by scripts/run-cell-multi.sh, which launches the claudecode and codex interactive drivers concurrently against ONE shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each. The workspace events.jsonl (shared by both cells) shows exactly the two-row, no-cross-contamination structure above: a claudecode session and a codex session, each keyed to the same cwd slug, each with its own independent working->ready arc, no flapping or merge. The daemon observed claudecode via its transcript fswatcher (~/.claude/projects) rather than hooks — the isolated daemon is not on :7837 where claudecode's hooks POST — which is sufficient for this scenario's per-session arcs. expected.jsonl is shared with the codex cell and is adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to working, agentB = the distinct second).
caveats: The recipe runs two different agent CLIs concurrently, so the rig (run-cell-multi.sh) needs both claude and codex installed and authenticated. ONE recording satisfies both matrix rows: the claudecode and codex cells share the workspace events.jsonl + expected.jsonl, differing only in which transcript each stores for its replay golden.
Adapter labels are stable strings (`claudecode`, `codex`) but display names can drift; the assertion in the recipe should be on the wire-level adapter name field, not the macOS display string.

sources.note: Parsers / PIDDiscoverers / ProcessNames / SubagentCounters are all keyed by adapter Identity.Name — there is no shared global parser or counter that could leak state between adapters.
Source-variant dispatch wires each adapter's FilesUnderRoot.Dir to its own fswatcher; the parser-factory map ensures lines under claudecode's root use the claudecode parser only.
Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace — three Expected bullets (correct adapter labels, independent transitions, no metrics/parent/subagent cross-contamination).

preconditions: claude AND codex CLIs on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other claudecode/codex session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches the claudecode and codex interactive drivers concurrently, both pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD
the isolated daemon observes claudecode via its transcript fswatcher (~/.claude/projects) and codex via ~/.codex/sessions — both keyed off the real $HOME — so it labels each adapter independently
this cell stores the claudecode transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha

verify: events.jsonl contains TWO distinct session_ids from two different adapters, both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the two adapters' rows

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

This scenario is about COEXISTENCE: one Codex session and one different-adapter session (e.g. claudecode) running concurrently in the SAME project directory, each shown with its correct adapter label and fully independent state + metrics.

### agent_supports: yes

Codex has no notion of, and imposes no constraint on, what other agent CLIs run in the same cwd. A `codex` process is an ordinary CLI that materializes its own transcript under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl` (see `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/adapter.go`, `defaultRootDir = ".codex/sessions"`). Nothing about launching a second, unrelated agent (claude, pi, aider) in the same directory interferes with a running Codex session. So the agent trivially "supports" coexistence — there is nothing for it to support or block.

### daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

The daemon labels each session by its adapter INDEPENDENTLY, by construction:

1. **Per-adapter process matching.** Codex registers `Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "codex"}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/agent.go`), while claudecode/pi/aider each register their own distinct matcher. The process scanner runs every adapter's matcher separately, so a `codex` process is tagged `codex` and a `claude` process is tagged `claudecode` — the adapter label is derived from which matcher hit, never from the cwd. Two different agents in one directory therefore get two correct, distinct labels.

2. **Disjoint transcript roots.** Codex's `Source` is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ".codex/sessions", ...}` (`agent.go`), whereas claudecode watches `~/.claude/projects/`. The fswatcher roots are per-adapter (derived from the `allAgents` slice), so a Codex transcript and a Claude transcript never land in the same watch root and cannot be confused or merged, regardless of sharing a cwd.

3. **Per-(adapter, session_id) metrics.** Each session's tokens / model / cost / state come from that adapter's own parser applied to that session's own transcript file. There is no cwd-keyed merging — sessions are keyed by their own UUID under their own adapter. A turn in the Codex session drives only the Codex transcript and thus only the Codex session's state arc; the claudecode session's state is unaffected, and vice versa.

This is the same independence the existing `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` capability relies on, generalized across DIFFERENT adapters rather than two instances of the same one. Because each adapter's matcher + watch root + parser are wired separately off the `allAgents` projections (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/maps.go`), independent labeling and metrics fall out of the architecture rather than needing special-case code.

## Recording (2026-05-24)

Recorded by scripts/run-cell-multi.sh: it launches the codex and claudecode interactive drivers concurrently against ONE shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each. The workspace events.jsonl (shared by both cells) shows the two-row independence above: a codex rollout session and a claudecode session, each keyed to the same cwd slug, each with its own working->ready arc, no cross-contamination or merge. ONE recording satisfies both the codex and the claudecode rows — they share events.jsonl + expected.jsonl and differ only in which transcript each stores for its replay golden. The scenario's requires:[headless_mode] gate is satisfied (capabilities.json → headless_mode: true).
caveats: Recorded by run-cell-multi.sh, which launches both adapters' drivers concurrently against one shared cwd under a single isolated --record daemon. The daemon observes claudecode via its transcript fswatcher (the isolated daemon is not on :7837 where claudecode's hooks POST), which suffices for this scenario's per-session working->ready arcs.

Independent labeling/metrics rely on the daemon scanning each adapter's process matcher and transcript root separately. If a future adapter ever shared a transcript root or process name with another, that assumption would need re-checking — not the case for codex today.

sources.note: Codex registers Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "codex"} and Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ".codex/sessions"} — per-adapter matcher + disjoint watch root
defaultRootDir = ".codex/sessions"; ProcessName = "codex" — distinct from claudecode's ~/.claude/projects/ root and process name
fswatcher roots, process scanners, and parser-factory map are all per-adapter projections of the allAgents slice — independent labeling/metrics fall out of the wiring

Cross-adapter recorder: launches both adapters' interactive drivers concurrently against one shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each (shared workspace events.jsonl, per-adapter transcript). This is the rig that unblocked the recording.
headless_mode: true — the scenario's requires gate is satisfied

irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other claudecode/codex session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches the claudecode and codex interactive drivers concurrently, both pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD

codex registers Process.Match=ExactName{Name:codex} + Source=FilesUnderRoot{Dir:.codex/sessions}, disjoint from claudecode's matcher and ~/.claude/projects root, so the daemon keys the two sessions independently
this cell stores the codex rollout for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: bravo

verify: events.jsonl contains TWO distinct session_ids from two different adapters, both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the two adapters' rows

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

Reassessment of a previously-`bug` cell against opencode v1.14.50 and the current daemon (opencode/watcher.go `scanSessions`, opencode/metrics.go). **The prior `daemon_capability: bug` was mislabeled and is now stale — it flips to `full`.**

## What this scenario actually tests

The primitive is *multi-adapter coexistence*: a DIFFERENT-adapter set (claudecode + codex + pi + aider) and one opencode session live in the SAME cwd at once, and the daemon must label each adapter independently with fully independent state/metrics and no cross-contamination. The user-observable signal is: each session appears with its correct adapter label; states transition independently; no merging/flapping of metrics, parent linkage, or subagent counts.

## Why the daemon is `full` here

The transports are disjoint at the source-variant level, so no shared reader/parser/table can leak state:
1. **Process matching** — opencode declares `ExactName{Name: opencode}` (agent.go); each adapter's matcher runs independently and tags each PID with its originating adapter.
2. **Session store** — opencode is the only `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter; its watcher keys every session by the SQLite `session.id` column (`ses_…`), NOT by cwd. `scanSessions` does `SELECT id, directory, … FROM session` and stores `w.cursors[s.id]` per id (watcher.go ~L257–L326); `ComputeMetrics` queries `WHERE p.session_id = ?` (metrics.go ~L67–L73). opencode.db only ever holds opencode rows, disjoint from the partners' FilesUnderRoot (claudecode/codex/pi JSONL) and FilesUnderCWD (aider markdown) transports.
3. **Per-(adapter, session_id) state** — metrics and parent linkage key off the (adapter, session_id) tuple via the maps.go projections; there is no global per-cwd aggregation.

The existing recording's `events.jsonl` is direct evidence the coexistence spec is met: five DISTINCT sessions appear, each with its correct adapter label — opencode `ses_19ea3aac2ffe5C0USJRj6zD2gL` (adapter opencode), claude-code `1dddc799-…`, codex `rollout-2026-05-26T00-58-19-…`, pi `2026-05-25T22-58-18-…`, aider `proc-28672` — each with its own independent ready→working transition (seq 735 for opencode, 723/736 for claude-code, 775/776 for codex, 786/787 for pi, 738/780 for aider). The daemon never merges or flaps one adapter's row into another's. This is the load-bearing assertion and it passes cleanly.

This matches the sibling `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` reassessment, which found the daemon keys opencode sessions by `session.id` (not cwd) and is therefore `daemon_capability: full` — two sessions are independently observable. The same keying makes two DIFFERENT adapters in one cwd trivially independent.

## Why the old `bug` was stale

The prior verdict downgraded this cell to `bug` over a single failing assertion: `agentB_turn_done` (the opencode session's working→ready settle). The recording shows WHY that one assertion truncated, and it is NOT a multi-adapter bug:
- opencode `ses_19ea3aac…` last `transcript_activity` at seq 804 (00:59:32.582);
- `process_exited` pid=28670 at seq 805 (00:59:32.722) — only ~140 ms later;
- the terminal `step-finish=stop` part is not read by the ProcessOwnedStore poller until seq 807 (00:59:38.094) — 5.5 s after the process already exited.

So there is no working→ready `state_transition` for the opencode row: the single-shot `opencode run` process exited before the poller's tick read the terminal part. This is opencode's GENERIC headless single-shot fast-exit truncation (the same mechanism captured for pi/turn-aborted-by-error and opencode's own headless settle behaviour), and the standalone `basic-turn` opencode fixture proves the daemon DOES emit working→ready when the poller's tick wins the race (opencode runs alone there → `turn1_done: ready`, no known_failing). It is therefore a settle-timing/driver concern, not a spec-required observable of the multi-adapter primitive — which the SKILL's authoring rule says belongs in `caveats`, keeping `daemon_capability: full`. Parking this generic settle-race in `bug` on the multi-agent cell is exactly the defensive-`bug` mislabel #476 set out to kill.

## Driver

The opencode interactive driver supports send/wait_turn/sleep and honors `$IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD`, so it shares the run-cell-multi.sh workspace with the partner adapters. The recipe (one short text turn) is exactly the shape this cell needs → `driver_capability: ready`. The agentB settle truncation is a property of single-shot `opencode run` exiting at turn-end; a persistent-session driver mode that outlives the settle would also clear the trailing arc, but it is not required for the coexistence assertions.

## Route

record — re-record (or re-promote) the cross-adapter fixture as a PASSING fixture for the load-bearing coexistence assertions and DROP the `known_failing` flag from expected.jsonl. The agentB working→ready settle is a caveat (opencode's headless fast-exit), not a daemon mishandling of this scenario.
caveats: opencode's `agentB_turn_done` working->ready settle can truncate: single-shot `opencode run` exits ~140 ms after the final transcript_activity (events.jsonl seq 805), before the ProcessOwnedStore poller reads the terminal step-finish=stop part (seq 807, +5.5 s). This is opencode's generic headless fast-exit settle race (cf. basic-turn captures it standalone when the poller's tick wins), NOT a multi-adapter merge/cross-contamination bug — the load-bearing coexistence assertions all pass.

opencode's default model (a slow local LM Studio model, ~30-45 s/turn) means its working span is long and it may enter working AFTER a faster partner; the spec is adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to working, agentB = the other), so either ordering validates.
Adapter labels are stable wire strings (opencode, claude-code, codex, pi, aider); the assertion is on the wire-level adapter name, not the macOS display string.

A persistent-session opencode driver mode (the -i TUI, not currently driver-supported) that outlives the turn would also clear the agentB settle arc — but it is not required for the coexistence primitive this cell tests.

sources.note: scanSessions SELECTs `id, directory, time_updated, parent_id FROM session` and keys cursors by `w.cursors[s.id]` (the SQLite session.id, ses_…), NOT by cwd — so two sessions (or a session next to a different adapter) in one cwd are independently observable. No state shared with any file-based adapter.
ComputeMetrics / querySessionMetrics query `WHERE p.session_id = ?` — metrics are per session.id, no per-cwd aggregation that could merge a partner adapter's tokens/model.

Five distinct adapter-labeled sessions each take an independent ready->working transition (opencode seq 735, claude-code 736, codex 775/776, pi 786/787, aider 780) with no merge/flap — the load-bearing coexistence assertion passes; daemon_capability=full.

Proof the single failing assertion is a headless fast-exit race, not a merge bug: opencode session last activity seq 804 (00:59:32.582) -> process_exited seq 805 (00:59:32.722, +140ms) -> terminal step-finish read seq 807 (00:59:38.094, +5.5s), so no working->ready state_transition was emitted for ses_19ea3aac. Belongs in caveats, not bug.

Standalone opencode turn1_done asserts expected_state=ready with NO known_failing — proves the daemon DOES emit working->ready when opencode runs alone (poller tick wins the race). Confirms the multi-agent settle truncation is a timing/driver concern, not daemon incapacity.
Sibling reassessment found the daemon keys opencode sessions by session.id (not cwd) -> daemon_capability: full. The same keying makes two DIFFERENT adapters in one cwd independent here.
Parsers / PIDDiscoverers / ProcessNames / MetricsProviders are keyed by adapter Identity.Name — opencode's MetricsReader is registered only for opencode sessions; disjoint dispatch per adapter.

preconditions: opencode AND at least one different-adapter CLI (claude, and the other concurrent adapters codex/pi/aider) on PATH and authenticated; opencode authenticates against its configured default model (a local LM Studio model in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json, so no remote key is needed)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
the opencode SQLite watcher is enabled in the recording daemon (ProcessOwnedStore source polling ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db)
no other opencode/claudecode/codex/pi/aider session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches each cross_adapter[] driver concurrently, all pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (the opencode interactive driver honors $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD — like the codex/pi/aider drivers — so it shares the workspace with the other adapters; opencode keys its SQLite session on session.directory = that shared cwd)

opencode registers Process.Match=ExactName{Name:opencode} + Source=ProcessOwnedStore{Reader over ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db}, the ONLY structured-store transport here — doubly disjoint from claudecode ~/.claude/projects, codex ~/.codex/sessions, pi ~/.pi/agent/sessions (FilesUnderRoot JSONL) and aider .aider.chat.history.md (FilesUnderCWD markdown). opencode.db never holds another adapter's rows, so the daemon keys the opencode session independently; its single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep) is sufficient because opencode runs ONE session next to a DIFFERENT adapter
this cell stores the opencode transcript (exported from the SQLite store) for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (every coexisting adapter)

the send is a single short text turn (one word) so opencode's working span carries no tool call and the session never enters waiting; opencode's default model is a small slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn), so the spec's working-anchor windows are generous and the trailing sleep must outlast the turn_done settle so working->ready is observed

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: epsilon

verify: events.jsonl contains the opencode session (label opencode, ses_… id) and at least one different-adapter session, each keyed to the same cwd slug

each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other (opencode's structured SQLite store is disjoint from every partner's file-based transport, so no shared state can leak)
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the opencode row into another adapter's; opencode's turn arc and any pid_discovered land on the ses_… session

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
applicable: yes — CROSS-ADAPTER cell, recorded by run-cell-multi.sh.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
applicable: yes — CROSS-ADAPTER cell, recorded by run-cell-multi.sh.

## Reasoning

This scenario is about COEXISTENCE: one pi session and one DIFFERENT-adapter session (claudecode) running concurrently in the SAME project directory, each shown with its correct adapter label and fully independent state + metrics. This is NOT `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` (two instances of the SAME agent — a driver_gap for pi because the pi driver is single-session and cannot mint a per-slot second pi process). Here pi drives exactly ONE session next to a different adapter, so pi's single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep) is sufficient and the per-adapter PID-binding weakness that downgraded multiple-sessions-same-cwd does not apply — the partner is a different process name in a different transcript root.

### agent_supports: yes

Pi has no notion of, and imposes no constraint on, what other agent CLIs run in the same cwd. A `pi` process is an ordinary CLI REPL that materializes its own transcript under `~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<uuid>.jsonl` (see `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/agent.go`, Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/}). Nothing about launching a second, unrelated agent (claude) in the same directory interferes with a running pi session. So the agent trivially "supports" coexistence — there is nothing for it to support or block.

### daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

The daemon labels each session by its adapter INDEPENDENTLY, by construction:

1. **Per-adapter process matching.** Pi registers `Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "pi"}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/agent.go`), while claudecode registers its own distinct matcher (~/.claude/projects watch + hook-based discovery). The process scanner runs every adapter's matcher separately (off the `allAgents` projections in `maps.go`), so a `pi` process is tagged `pi` and a `claude` process is tagged `claudecode` — the adapter label derives from which matcher hit, never from the cwd. Two different agents in one directory therefore get two correct, distinct labels.

2. **Disjoint transcript roots.** Pi's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ".pi/agent/sessions"}` whereas claudecode watches `~/.claude/projects/`. The fswatcher roots are per-adapter (derived from the `allAgents` slice), so a pi transcript and a claude transcript never land in the same watch root and cannot be confused or merged, regardless of sharing a cwd.

3. **Per-(adapter, session_id) metrics + fresh parser per file.** Pi's Source instantiates a FRESH stateful `Parser{}` per transcript file (JSONLineParser{NewParser}), so the pi session never shares parser state with the claudecode session — a turn on the pi transcript drives only the pi session's state arc; the claudecode session's state is unaffected, and vice versa. There is no cwd-keyed merging — sessions are keyed by their own UUID under their own adapter.

The load-bearing PID-binding weakness from `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` (pi's DiscoverPIDByCWD cannot disambiguate two SAME-cwd SAME-adapter pi processes) does NOT bite here: the two coexisting processes have DIFFERENT process names (`pi` vs `claude`), so each adapter's pgrep-x matcher returns a disjoint candidate set and binds its own PID cleanly. Independent labeling/metrics fall out of the architecture rather than needing special-case code, the same way the codex/claudecode cross-adapter cell relies on it.

## Recording (2026-05-25)

Recorded by scripts/run-cell-multi.sh: it reads the scenario's cross_adapter[] list, launches the pi and claudecode interactive drivers CONCURRENTLY against ONE shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon (coexisting with production on an alternate port + IRRLICHT_HOME), then curates one per-adapter fixture each. The workspace events.jsonl (shared by the rows) shows the two-row independence above: a pi session and a claudecode session, each keyed to the same cwd slug, each with its own working->ready arc, no cross-contamination or merge. The pi cell stores the pi transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl + expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters). The scenario's requires:[headless_mode] gate is satisfied (capabilities.json → headless_mode: true).

The pi interactive driver needed a one-line ABI-parity change to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (it previously hardcoded $STAGING/cwd), matching the claudecode/codex drivers — that lets both adapters share one workspace. This is the cross-adapter shared-cwd convention, NOT a new step type; pi still drives a single session with its existing send/wait_turn/sleep steps, so this is not a driver_gap.
caveats: Recorded by run-cell-multi.sh, which launches both adapters' drivers concurrently against one shared cwd under a single isolated --record daemon. The daemon observes claudecode via its transcript fswatcher (the isolated daemon is not on :7837 where claudecode's hooks POST), which suffices for this scenario's per-session working->ready arcs; pi is observed via its own ~/.pi/agent/sessions transcript fswatcher.

Distinct from multiple-sessions-same-cwd: that cell is applicable:false for pi (driver_gap — the single-session pi driver can't launch two concurrent SAME-cwd pi sessions, and DiscoverPIDByCWD can't disambiguate them). This cell pairs pi with a DIFFERENT adapter, so pi drives one session and the per-adapter independence holds.

pi mints its own session UUID (session_id_assignment:false), so — like codex and unlike claudecode — a recipe cannot pre-pin the session id; the spec asserts two DISTINCT sessions via new_session/same_session_as relations.

Every expected.jsonl phase anchors on a working transition, never a ready: pi's process scanner (ExactName{pi}, pgrep -x) emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready before the transcript is discovered; a ready-anchored new_session phase would greedily bind to that placeholder.

The pi interactive driver was given a one-line change to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (cross-adapter shared-cwd convention, matching claudecode/codex). Not a new step type — pi still uses only send/wait_turn/sleep.

sources.note: Pi registers Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "pi"} (pgrep -x) and Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.pi/agent/sessions, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser}} — per-adapter matcher + disjoint watch root + fresh stateful parser per transcript file.
fswatcher roots, process scanners, and parser-factory map are all per-adapter projections of the allAgents slice — independent labeling/metrics across DIFFERENT adapters fall out of the wiring.

DiscoverPID = DiscoverPIDByCWD("pi", ...). The cwd-only narrowing weakness only bites two SAME-adapter pi processes in one cwd; here the partner is a DIFFERENT process name (claude), so each adapter's matcher returns a disjoint PID set and binds cleanly.

Cross-adapter recorder: reads cross_adapter[], launches each adapter's interactive driver concurrently against one shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each (shared workspace events.jsonl, per-adapter transcript).

Single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep/interrupt). Given a one-line change to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD so it can share a workspace with a different adapter — the cross-adapter shared-cwd convention, not a new step type.
Sibling cross-adapter cell (codex paired with claudecode, yes/yes). Same architecture argument and same spec shape; the pi spec is adapted from it.
headless_mode:true (scenario requires it); session_id_assignment:false (pi mints its own UUID — recipe cannot pre-pin the id); subagents:false, permission_hooks:false.

preconditions: pi AND claude (and codex, the third concurrent adapter) CLIs on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other pi/claudecode/codex session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches each cross_adapter[] driver concurrently, all pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (the pi interactive driver honors $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD so it shares the workspace with the other adapters)

pi registers Process.Match=ExactName{Name:pi} + Source=FilesUnderRoot{Dir:.pi/agent/sessions} with a fresh stateful Parser per transcript, disjoint from claudecode's ~/.claude/projects and codex's ~/.codex/sessions roots, so the daemon keys the pi session independently — its single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep) is sufficient because pi runs ONE session next to a DIFFERENT adapter (distinct from multiple-sessions-same-cwd, which needs two SAME-adapter pi sessions and is a driver_gap)
this cell stores the pi transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (every coexisting adapter)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: gamma

verify: events.jsonl contains the pi session and at least one different-adapter session, each keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other (pi's fresh per-transcript parser guarantees no shared state)
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the pi row into another adapter's
